seoTitle: How to View Epics in a Jira Kanban Board
metaDescription: Learn how to surface epics directly on a Jira Kanban board so you can see which larger initiative each card belongs to at a glance.
intro: If your Kanban board is full of cards but you can't tell which epic each one rolls up to, Jira has a built-in way to show that context right on the board. This walkthrough covers how to turn epic visibility on and use it while you're triaging or planning work.

steps.heading: Open your Kanban board
steps.body: Navigate to the project's Kanban board from the sidebar. This is where the epic panel and card details live.

steps.heading: Enable the epic panel or swimlanes
steps.body: Use the board settings to turn on the epic panel (or group swimlanes by epic), which lists every epic in the project alongside the board.

steps.heading: Select an epic to filter the board
steps.body: Click an epic in the panel to highlight or filter the cards that belong to it, so you can focus on just that slice of work.

steps.heading: Check the epic label on each card
steps.body: Cards typically show a small colored epic tag; use this to quickly identify an epic without opening the panel at all.

steps.heading: Clear the filter when you're done
steps.body: Deselect the epic to return to the full board view before moving on to other planning tasks.

faq.question: Why don't I see any epics on my Kanban board?
faq.answer: The epic panel may be turned off in board settings, or no issues on the board have been assigned to an epic yet.

faq.question: Can I filter the board to show only one epic?
faq.answer: Yes, clicking an epic in the panel filters the board to just the cards linked to it.

faq.question: Does this work the same way on Scrum boards?
faq.answer: Scrum boards offer a very similar epic panel, though the layout differs slightly from Kanban.

faq.question: Do I need admin permissions to enable epic visibility?
faq.answer: No, board display settings like the epic panel are usually available to any board viewer.
